artifact: PF-REF3 repaired official Palantir parity-reference source ledger
source_policy: Only direct, live-reopened pages under https://www.palantir.com/docs/foundry/. Each claim is limited to the stated page section. The ledger is reference evidence, not a product-parity claim.
retrieval_policy: Every URL below was reopened live on 2026-07-19 and returned HTTP 200. source_date_status is recorded per page: rendered footer/copyright years and crawler dates are not treated as a page publication or update date.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Current page reachable; no page-level release/SLA is inferred.

section_claim_mappings.claim: The introductory model distinguishes Ontology objects, links and actions from datasets and their lineage.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Duplicate-key behavior differs between Ontology Storage versions; do not generalize a universal deterministic-rejection contract.

section_claim_mappings.claim: Object-type primary keys are configured against backing data and the page documents key-quality/duplicate-key caveats.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Base-type and action-surface limitations remain scoped to the documented property features.

section_claim_mappings.claim: Properties have identifiers, display/lifecycle metadata and rendering/query-related configuration with documented type limitations.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Cross-Ontology links are documented as unsupported; this is not a cross-tenant or security-boundary declaration.

section_claim_mappings.claim: A link type defines a relationship between object types with independently traversable sides.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Temporary object sets expire within the documented window; no broader persistence commitment is inferred.

section_claim_mappings.claim: Object Set Service supports static/dynamic and temporary/permanent object sets.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Several interface capabilities are beta, under active development, or unsupported by language/application surface.

section_claim_mappings.claim: Interfaces are abstract contracts implemented by object types, with explicitly uneven support across surfaces.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Mappings/search behavior is only the documented interface feature scope.

section_claim_mappings.claim: Implementations map required interface properties, links and constrained action behavior; interface search returns matching implementers.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Criteria are separate from permissions and do not declare idempotency, exactly-once, or audit guarantees.

section_claim_mappings.claim: Action submission conditions combine user, parameter, object and relation context into a logical statement.

sources.current_availability_qualification: This page's distinct legacy caveat is that Action-type authorization is changing from legacy permissions to Ontology roles; it does not establish P12's project-permission migration conditions.

section_claim_mappings.claim: Applying an action requires the documented visibility/datasource access, passing submission criteria, and—where edits are not actions-only—the applicable writeback edit permission or policy.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Action logs are configured object types; they are not documentation of rejected-command logs, immutability, WORM, or tamper evidence.

section_claim_mappings.section: Action log Ontology; Action log schema
section_claim_mappings.claim: A configured action log creates one log object per submitted action and can record action/version, timestamp, submitting user and edited primary keys.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Function overview documents platform capabilities only; no blanket timeout, audit, identity, exactly-once, or cross-system transaction guarantee is asserted.

section_claim_mappings.claim: Foundry functions run server-side TypeScript/Python logic that can read Ontology data, traverse links and make Ontology edits.

sources.current_availability_qualification: This page's distinct migration/enrollment caveat: project-based permissioning is enabled for new Ontologies; existing resources require owner enablement/migration; Default Ontologies are excluded. Object visibility remains dependent on configured object/data security.

section_claim_mappings.section: Ontology permissions; Viewing object types and objects
section_claim_mappings.claim: Project permissions govern resources, while viewing object instances requires object-type permission plus the configured data/object/property access.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Atomicity is scoped to a dataset transaction/latest dataset view; no cross-resource or external-effect atomicity is inferred.

section_claim_mappings.claim: Dataset transactions move OPEN to COMMITTED or ABORTED; committed writes enter the latest view, aborted writes are ignored; SNAPSHOT, APPEND, UPDATE and DELETE are documented.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Branch/fallback resolution does not declare complete merge diffs or deterministic conflict behavior.

section_claim_mappings.claim: Dataset branches are transaction-sequence pointers; builds use a branch and fallback chain to resolve data/logic.

sources.current_availability_qualification: High-level integration overview only; detailed build/pipeline acceptance semantics are sourced separately.

section_claim_mappings.claim: Foundry describes integrated security, lineage, transformation, change-management, quality and scheduling capabilities.

sources.current_availability_qualification: The page supports interactive lineage exploration, not record-level completeness or a universal provenance-SLA assertion.

section_claim_mappings.claim: Lineage supports ancestor/descendant navigation and inspection of dataset schema, builds and code, with shareable snapshots.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Pending-trigger and dispatch semantics do not establish exact queue cardinality or completed-work semantics.

section_claim_mappings.claim: Schedules trigger builds; a trigger during an active run is pending, and a successful schedule run means build initiation rather than build completion.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Automate is presented as the backwards-compatible replacement for Object Monitoring; its documented effects do not guarantee dedupe, retries, or exactly-once irreversible effects.

section_claim_mappings.claim: Automate can evaluate time/object conditions continuously or on a schedule and submit actions, invoke functions, or notify users.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Configured platform/email notification behavior is documented; delivery SLO, recovery-event behavior and ownership model are not.

section_claim_mappings.claim: Health checks monitor dataset, schedule and table conditions including time, size, content and schema and can notify through platform/email.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Retention sweep timing is variable and recovery becomes unavailable after sweep; legal-hold precedence is not stated.

section_claim_mappings.claim: Retention marks dataset transactions, normally preserves the latest branch view, and later sweeps marked transactions.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Availability/policy overrides are material; DSR or legal-hold behavior is not established.

section_claim_mappings.claim: Data Lifetime is a lineage-aware deletion mechanism that propagates deletion to downstream derived transactions.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Language support differs; live preview runs in a different runtime from published functions and permissions may take time to propagate.

section_claim_mappings.section: Test in live preview; Publish your functions
section_claim_mappings.claim: A functions repository supports live preview before commit; a tag/release publishes functions to the registry after its checks pass.

sources.current_availability_qualification: A failed job can leave previously completed jobs' output written; build locking is output-dataset scoped, not a universal all-output rollback guarantee.

section_claim_mappings.section: Jobs and JobSpecs; Job states; Build lifecycle; Live logs
section_claim_mappings.claim: Builds compute dataset versions through jobs with WAITING/RUN_PENDING/RUNNING/ABORT_PENDING/ABORTED/FAILED/COMPLETED states; resolution validates inputs, opens output transactions for build locking, may queue input-conflicting builds, and exposes live job logs.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Streaming pipelines are not available on all Foundry environments; integrity checks prevent documented output failures, not every semantic/data-quality failure.

section_claim_mappings.claim: Pipeline Builder provides graph/form pipeline creation, pipeline-integrity and output checks, full version control, and an inputs-transform-preview-deliver-outputs workflow.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Python UDFs are recommended for the described experience; Java UDF availability and all output/stream choices are conditional on the documented product context.

section_claim_mappings.section: Using transforms in Pipeline Builder; User-defined functions
section_claim_mappings.claim: Expressions transform columns, transforms accept/return tables, joins/unions have specific configuration, and reusable UDFs can be versioned and upgraded.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Workflow Lineage requires application access enabled by an administrator and is complementary to, not interchangeable with, Pipeline Builder and Data Lineage.

section_claim_mappings.section: Workflow Lineage; When to use; Access control
section_claim_mappings.claim: Workflow Lineage explores ontology workflow resources and their API names, inputs, edits, criteria and code snippets; it can show outdated functions/permissions and bulk-update action versions.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Cross-Ontology graph display has stated limited functionality; action bulk updates are constrained to the current Ontology/function-backed actions.

section_claim_mappings.section: Getting started; Support for multiple ontologies
section_claim_mappings.claim: Workflow Lineage can be opened from listed applications and displays relevant cross-Ontology resources with documented limitations.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Metrics/history/log retention and access are explicitly windowed/permissioned; observability does not declare audit immutability or 100% delivery.

section_claim_mappings.section: Observability; Monitoring; Metrics; Debugging; Log export
section_claim_mappings.claim: The platform documents monitoring rules/alerts, function-action-AIP metrics, Workflow Lineage execution history/log search, trace views, and log/metric/trace export.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Run history covers the documented 30-day window; Pipeline Builder sidecar UDFs are explicitly excluded from execution history.

section_claim_mappings.section: Run history data; Limitations; Filter run history
section_claim_mappings.claim: Function, Action and Automation history shows execution timestamp/status/runtime/caller/source executor with filters, log-detail inspection, and a stated UDF limitation.

sources.current_availability_qualification: The page documents audit-log content/access and SIEM ingestion, not WORM, hash chaining, immutable retention or a legal-hold contract.

section_claim_mappings.section: Audit logs; What audit logs capture
section_claim_mappings.claim: Foundry audit logs record who/what/when/where details for platform actions and can be consumed by external SIEMs through the documented audit API/storage mechanism.

sources.current_availability_qualification: Log exporting is Beta/enrollment-admin gated; logs are not audit logs, 100% delivery is not guaranteed, contents may change, and access/retention conditions apply.

section_claim_mappings.claim: Organization administrators can configure streamed telemetry logs containing application/function, transform/pipeline, build/deployment and error events; the page expressly limits log guarantees.
